title: Optional Tauri tray client design
objective: Implement the second-stage optional Tauri tray client without making it mandatory for all users.
non_goals: Do not implement Tauri before stage 1 server queue is stable.
Do not store user passwords in the client.
Do not store full workorder data locally.
Do not require administrator privileges for normal client operation.
Do not support macOS unless separately requested.
requirements: Device registration must use a one-time code or equivalent explicit linking flow.
Device token must be scoped and revocable.
The client must show tray icon, status and open-portal action.
Notifications must open target URLs in the system browser.
Local logs must avoid tokens and sensitive notification bodies.
Windows and Linux packaging requirements must be documented.
acceptance_checks: A user can link a device and see it in the portal.
Revoking the device stops notification feed access.
The client can receive a notification while the browser is closed.
Clicking a notification opens the portal URL.
Build documentation covers Windows and Linux.
